title: Trivial properties should be auto-implemented
description: Trivial properties should be auto-implementedAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellclumsyWhy is this an issue?Trivial properties, which include no logic but setting and getting a backing field should be converted to auto-implemented properties, yielding cleaner and more readable code.Noncompliant code examplepublic class Car { private string _make; public string Make // Noncompliant { get { return _make; } set { _make = value; } } }Compliant solutionpublic class Car { public string Make { get; set; } }Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since9.1Analyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSou
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

compliant: public int Age { get; set; } // Compliant - auto-implemented

checks.message: Trivial property detected. Consider using auto-implemented property syntax: { get; set; }

never_trigger_on: Properties with validation logic in setter
Properties with logic in getter (null coalescing, computation)
Properties with side effects (events, method calls)
logic.reasoning: Trivial properties that simply get and set a backing field without any logic should be converted to auto-implemented properties for cleaner, more readable code. This rule detects the pattern of 'get { return _field; } set { _field = value; }' which can be simplified to '{ get; set; }'.
